title: Sequential Circuits: State, Clock and Types
summary: Introduces sequential circuits: how internal state is stored and evolves, clock signals (synchronous vs. asynchronous operation), and the two main types (bistable and multi-bit).
content_points: Output depends on both input and internal state; same input can yield different outputs at different times.
Synchronous: state changes on clock edge/level; asynchronous: state changes on any event.
Clock period must be long enough for signals to stabilise through all gates.
Level-active: responds while clock is at level; edge-active: responds on clock transition.
Feedback is essential for memory in sequential circuits.
learning_objectives: Explain the role of a clock in synchronous circuits
